title: Do not copy up when volume is not empty
body:
When Docker performs a copy up, it first verifies that the volume being copied into is empty; thus, for volumes that have been modified elsewhere (e.g. manually copying into then), the copy up will not be performed at all. Duplicate this behavior in Podman by checking if the volume is empty before copying.

Furthermore, move setting copyup to false further up. This will prevent a potential race where copy up could happen more than once if Podman was killed after some files had been copied but before the DB was updated.

I confirm dd65ad5a fixes the issue. Here is a reproducer:

```
# step1: create the image
CTX=$(buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:31)
buildah run $CTX sh -c 'mkdir /data; echo oops > /data/secret.txt'
buildah commit --rm $CTX fedora-data

# step2: create the volume
podman volume create test
echo readonly-file > ~/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test/_data/secret.txt

# step3: trigger issue
podman run -it --rm --volume test:/data:ro fedora-data id
cat ~/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/test/_data/secret.txt
```

The last stdout line should be `readonly-file`, not `oops`
Tests seem to be coming back as flakes, not failures, this time. Might be ready with some retesting.
Nevermind, need to fix one thing in tests
Re-pushed, should be good now. Held LGTM would be appreciated.
